channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: The Gleiwitz Radio Tower: The Site of a Fake Attack That Triggered WWII
item.description: The Gleiwitz radio station, located near the German-Polish border, is known for being the site of a dramatic incident that led to the outbreak of World War II. On August 31, 1939, Nazi operatives stag...

item.title: Drones Revolutionize Winter Olympics Coverage with Dynamic, Close-Up Action Shots
item.description: The 2026 Winter Olympics in Milan and Cortina marked a turning point in sports broadcasting with the introduction of FPV drones, offering an immersive experience for viewers. Unlike traditional, dista...

item.title: Mullvad's Anti-Censorship Ads Banned on UK Television Amid Rising Surveillance
item.description: Mullvad, a VPN provider, faced censorship in the United Kingdom after attempting to broadcast its TV commercial 'And Then?', directed by Jonas Åkerlund, which criticized mass surveillance and censorsh...

item.title: Connections Between High-Profile PR Firms, Johnny Depp, and Justin Baldoni Highlighted in Epstein Files
item.description: Recent scrutiny of the Jeffrey Epstein files has revealed indirect connections between high-profile celebrities Johnny Depp and Justin Baldoni and crisis publicists previously linked to Epstein. Depar...

item.title: UK Couple Receives $200,000 Medical Bill Following Premature Birth in the US
item.description: UK folk musicians Issy Ferris and Archie Sylvester faced a $200,000 medical bill after their son, Lucky, was born prematurely at 33 weeks during their tour in Nashville. Issy experienced severe sympto...

item.title: US Ambassador Huckabee Says Biblical Borders Would Make Israeli Expansion 'Fine' in Carlson Interview
item.description: In a February 2026 interview with Tucker Carlson, US Ambassador to Israel Mike Huckabee expressed that it would be acceptable for Israel to control territory spanning from the Nile to the Euphrates, a...

item.title: Trial of Captain of 'Ghost Fleet' Ship to Take Place Tomorrow
item.description: Tomorrow, a new trial will take place for the captain of the Boracay, a ship accused of being part of the 'Russian ghost fleet.' Initially, accusations against the captain revolved around economic san...

item.title: Feuerwasser Wood-Fired Garden Shower: A Simple, Elemental Outdoor Bath
item.description: The Feuerwasser Garden Shower offers a unique outdoor bathing experience that combines the elements of fire and water without relying on electricity, gas, or solar energy. Designed around a patented s...

item.title: Kim Delaney Responds to Lawsuit Over 2022 Motorcycle Accident
item.description: Actress Kim Delaney, known for her roles on All My Children and General Hospital, has denied allegations that she was driving under the influence during a 2022 motorcycle accident. The incident involv...

item.title: Small Business Owner Appeals to Trump for Release of Detained Fiancé
item.description: Wayne DeMario, a small business owner who voted for Donald Trump, is making a public plea to the President for help after his fiancé, Yamile Alcantu, was detained by U.S. Immigration and Customs Enfor...

item.title: NASA Reports Serious Safety Issues in Starliner CFT Crewed Mission
item.description: The Starliner CFT mission, Boeing's first crewed flight of its Starliner spacecraft, faced significant safety issues, classified as a 'Type A Incident' by NASA—the agency's most severe category, used ...

item.title: European Activists Who Dedicated Their Lives to the Palestinian Cause
item.description: This documentary episode explores the lives and sacrifices of two European individuals who devoted themselves to supporting the Palestinian cause. Francoise Kesteman, a French nurse and committed comm...

item.title: Trump’s Glyphosate Production Order Sparks Backlash Within MAHA Coalition Ahead of Midterms
item.description: Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. publicly defended President Donald Trump’s executive order invoking the Defense Production Act to expand domestic production of glyphosate and...

item.title: Palantir Reports $1.5 Billion in U.S. Income but Owes No Federal Tax After Using Research Deduction
item.description: A report by the Institute on Taxation and Economic Policy (ITEP) states that Palantir Technologies paid no federal corporate income tax in fiscal year 2025, despite reporting $1.5 billion in U.S. inco...

item.title: Iran, China, and Russia Formalize Strategic Cooperation Without Military Alliance
item.description: On February 2, 2026, Iran, China, and Russia signed a new strategic agreement that strengthens their diplomatic, economic, and security coordination. Unlike previous bilateral agreements—such as Iran-...

item.title: Claims About Epstein, Gates and ResearchGate’s Funding and Influence on Scientific Publishing
item.description: The article alleges that Jeffrey Epstein and Bill Gates played a significant role in financing ResearchGate, an academic social network where researchers share papers and collaborate. It argues that t...

item.title: Call for a Permanent and Affordable Public Housing System in Spain
item.description: The article argues that Spain’s housing crisis is not a temporary problem but a structural consequence of a capitalist economic model in which housing functions primarily as a vehicle for profit. It d...

item.title: Hungary Considers Expanding Energy Restrictions on Ukraine Amid Oil Transit Dispute
item.description: The Hungarian government is reportedly considering the possibility of cutting off gas and electricity supplies to Ukraine following escalating tensions between the two countries over energy transit. A...

item.title: Georgia Elections Board Reprimands Elon Musk’s PAC for Sending Prefilled Ballot Applications
item.description: The Georgia State Elections Board has formally reprimanded Elon Musk's America PAC after discovering that it sent prefilled absentee ballot applications to voters in violation of state law. The law pr...

item.title: The Life and Legacy of Józef Antos’ Unique Wind Turbine in Poland
item.description: Józef Antos, born in 1932 in southern Poland, dedicated his life to the ambitious goal of producing electricity from wind for his country. After graduating from a local mining school, he became a skil...
